div.h2: Syscall Timing: What You Actually Measure vs True Cost

p.em: ptrace introduces 50–500× overhead per syscall — useful for relative comparison, not absolute measurement

g.text: What ptrace Timing IS vs IS NOT Good For
Why eBPF/perf_events Exist — Direct Engineering Response
T+20ns | Ring-3→Ring-0: MSR_LSTAR jump, stack switch
T+100ns | User: rax = PID value, execution continues
TLB impact | ~0ns | same page table (no switch)
Context switch | 0 | none — stays in process
TOTAL TRUE COST | ~100ns | 1 cache line of work
T+0.5μs | [KERNEL] TASK_TRACED: context switch to TRACER
T+2.5μs | [KERNEL] context switch back to TRACEE
T+3.0μs | [TRACEE] kernel executes getpid() — ~100ns real work
T+3.1μs | [KERNEL] syscall done: stop tracee again (exit stop)
T+3.6μs | [KERNEL] context switch to TRACER again
Actual getpid() work | ~0.1μs | 0.1ns true cost hidden here
TLB pressure | ~0.3μs | CR3 switches flush TLB
Range: fast=1μs (TLB warm) → slow=50μs (heavy scheduler load)
→ read() does 10× more REAL work (disk I/O wait)
Blocked syscalls (read waiting on disk) dominate
Hot syscall identification → ratios are reliable
Absolute ns values are tracer overhead, not syscall cost
Cannot compare to un-traced runs (10× program slowdown)
Cannot compare to hardware counter measurements
ltrace | 2–100μs | ptrace + PLT hooks | 2+ per call
perf stat | ~10ns | PMU counters | 0 per syscall
eBPF tracepoint | ~200ns | in-kernel BPF VM | 0 per syscall
ptrace: syscall → stop tracee → wake tracer → read regs → resume
eBPF: syscall → kernel runs BPF program inline → continue
BPF program runs in ring-0, same context as the syscall handler
BPF writes to ring buffer shared with userspace (no ptrace ioctls)
Userspace reads ring buffer asynchronously — no per-event wakeup
→ counts openat with ~0.01% overhead vs ptrace's 10× slowdown
perf stat → absolute syscall counts with hardware precision
eBPF/bpftrace → production profiling, low overhead tracing
NEVER use ptrace absolute times to benchmark syscall cost
ALWAYS state overhead when sharing ptrace timing data
The 43× inflation is not a bug — it is fundamental to ptrace
